goal: Point him at built-in Isaac Sim robot library (Franka, UR10e, Nova Carter, Jetbot) instead of sketchy GitHub URDFs.
thoughts: Isaac Sim 5.x ships a curated robot library in the assets browser -- no need to scrape GitHub. List canonical starters. list_scene_templates gives one-click scenes with robots already placed. URDF import is 'for later', not the starting path.
code:
templates = list_scene_templates(category="robots")
kb = lookup_knowledge(
    query="Isaac Sim 5.x built-in robot asset library Franka UR10e Nova Carter Jetbot"
)
# Option A: drop a robot into empty scene
create_prim(path="/World/Robot", type_name="Xform")
add_reference(
    prim_path="/World/Robot",
    reference_path="<FRANKA_USD_FROM_ASSET_LIB>"
)
# Option B: full scene with robot placed + physics
load_scene_template(template_name="<FRANKA_STARTER>")
# Advanced (later): import_robot(source_path="<URDF>", ...)
failure_modes: Sending him to random GitHub URDF repos for quality-unknown assets
Telling him to model a robot in Blender
Starting with URDF import when he just wants a working arm on screen
